parameters.name: active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: While on, the receives information sent to the network port. While Off, no updating occurs. Data sent to the port is lost.

parameters.name: protocol
parameters.label: Protocol
parameters.description: Select which protocol to use, refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: address
parameters.label: Network Address
parameters.description: For multi-cast protocol, this is the multi-cast address to listen for. For UDT protocol this is the IP address of the server.

parameters.name: port
parameters.label: Port
parameters.description: The port which OSC-In will accept packets on.

parameters.name: localaddress
parameters.label: Local Address
parameters.description: Specify an IP address to receive on, useful when the system has mulitple NICs (Network Interface Card) and you want to select which one to use.

parameters.name: shared
parameters.label: Shared Connection
parameters.description: Use the same connection as other networking DATs using the same network protocol.

parameters.name: addscope
parameters.label: OSC Address Scope
parameters.description: To reduce which message are generated, you can use message address name patterns to include or exclude messages. For example, will exclude accelerometer messages coming in from an iOS or iPhone app li

parameters.name: typetag
parameters.label: Include Type Tag
parameters.description: Includes the argument list type tag in each message. It includes the parameter type keywords (in case the parsing application needs to identify parameter types).

parameters.name: splitbundle
parameters.label: Split Bundle into Messages
parameters.description: When On, each message contained within a bundle is given its own row.

parameters.name: splitmessage
parameters.label: Split Message into Columns
parameters.description: When On, OSC address and arguments are given individual columns, otherwise they are included in the message column.

parameters.name: bundletimestamp
parameters.label: Bundle Timestamp Column
parameters.description: When On, each bundle timestamp value is included in a column.

parameters.name: callbacks
parameters.label: Callbacks DAT
parameters.description: The Callbacks will execute once for each message received. See for usage.

parameters.name: executeloc
parameters.label: Execute from
parameters.description: Determines the location the script is run from.

parameters.name: fromop
parameters.label: From Operator
parameters.description: The operator whose state change will trigger the to execute its script when is set to . This operator is also the path that the script will be executed from if the parameter is set to .

parameters.name: clamp
parameters.label: Clamp Output
parameters.description: The is limited to 100 messages by default but with Clamp Output, this can be set to anything including unlimited.

parameters.name: maxlines
parameters.label: Maximum Lines
parameters.description: Limits the number of messages, older messages are removed from the list first.

parameters.name: clear
parameters.label: Clear Output
parameters.description: Deletes all lines except the heading. To clear with a python script

parameters.name: bytes
parameters.label: Bytes Column
parameters.description: Outputs the raw bytes of the message in a separate column.

parameters.name: language
parameters.label: Language
parameters.description: Select how the decides which script language to operate on.

parameters.name: extension
parameters.label: Edit/View Extension
parameters.description: Select the file extension this should expose to external editors.

parameters.name: customext
parameters.label: Custom Extension
parameters.description: Specifiy the custom extension.

parameters.name: wordwrap
parameters.label: Word Wrap
parameters.description: Enable Word Wrap for Node Display.

examples.label: Configure Language and Port
examples.description: Common settings from 5 real Derivative examples
